name: Municipal Trust And Savings Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Kankakee County since 1971, offering personal and business banking, mortgages, and deposit products through branches in Bourbonnais and Momence, Illinois.
description_long:
Municipal Trust and Savings Bank is a locally-focused community bank that has been operating since 1971, making it over 50 years old. The bank is deeply rooted in Kankakee County, Illinois, with a stated mission to serve as community banking and mortgage experts. The institution has received recognition as the #1 bank in its category for two consecutive years, suggesting strong customer satisfaction within its service area.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, business checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), individual retirement accounts (IRAs), credit cards, and home mortgage financing. On the mortgage side, they provide purchase mortgages, refinancing options, and partner with MyHome for additional financing solutions. They also offer specialized services including land trusts with FAQs and form downloads, suggesting expertise in estate planning and property management structures.

Municipal Bank distinguishes itself through personalized service, maintaining multiple NMLS-licensed loan officers (Marsha Lloyd, Cathy Boicken, Jasmine Strohm, and Lori Weir) available for individual consultation. The bank provides an online mortgage center with current rates, loan calculators, and secure online applications, combining traditional community banking with modern digital tools. Their focus on land trusts and estate-related services sets them apart from larger regional competitors.

As a traditional community bank, Municipal Trust and Savings Bank is best suited for customers in Kankakee County seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making. However, their geographic limitation to two branch locations and apparent lack of extensive online-only banking capabilities may limit appeal for remote customers or those seeking cutting-edge fintech solutions. The bank appears solid for mortgage products and community-focused financial needs but may have limited product depth compared to larger regional institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online mortgage center with rate quotes
pros: Established 50+ year history since 1971 with deep community roots in Kankakee County
Voted #1 for two consecutive years, indicating strong local customer satisfaction
Multiple NMLS-licensed mortgage officers available for personalized loan consultation
Online mortgage center with rate quotes, loan calculators, and secure applications
Specialized expertise in land trusts with dedicated resources and form downloads
Offers both personal and business banking products with competitive rates displayed
Routing number and NMLS# (422377) publicly listed, indicating regulatory compliance
cons: Limited to two physical branch locations (Bourbonnais and Momence), restricting accessibility for broader market
No mention of mobile app, mobile banking, or mobile deposit capabilities on website
Lacks information about fee structures, minimum balance requirements, or APY specifics in provided content
No indication of 24/7 customer support, with only standard branch hours mentioned
Limited digital product offerings compared to regional and national competitors
best_for: Kankakee County residents seeking relationship-based community banking with local branch access
Homebuyers and property owners in Illinois interested in personalized mortgage consultation
Small business owners in the service area needing business checking and lending relationships
Individuals requiring estate planning services through land trust expertise
diagnosis: Municipal Trust and Savings Bank is best for Kankakee County residents and small business owners who value personalized, relationship-based banking and have local branch access needs. The main caveat is their geographic limitation to two branches and apparent lack of robust digital-first banking options, making them less suitable for tech-savvy or geographically dispersed customers seeking modern online-only banking solutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community bank in Kankakee County since 1971, offering personal and business banking, mortgages, and deposit products.
